decisions_or_constraints: Artifact-only lane: no source, test, package script, or implementation file changes were made.

Shared quality-gate surfaces must be treated as serial join surfaces: scripts/quality-gate/types.ts, scripts/quality-gate/modes.ts, scripts/quality-gate/runner.ts, package.json, and scripts/quality-gate/__tests__/routing.test.ts.
[P] is valid only for an executable lane-level task whose write_scope is isolated from every other ready task in the same batch and whose dependencies are stable.
Read-only guardrail tasks can run together, but they should not use [P] as an implementation marker unless the task package clearly labels the batch as read-only.

write_set_decisions.decision: Read-only guardrail. Parallel with T002 is safe, but parallel_safe false is conservative and acceptable.
Read-only guardrail. Parallel with T001 is safe.
Must run before any downstream task depending on new QualityGateMode. Do not batch with T012, T016, or T019 because all modify types.ts even though they occur in later phases.
Serial shared-surface join for fast mode definition. Must complete before package script or later lane additions.
Isolated package script edit after T004. Parallel marker is not useful in the current graph because it has no same-batch peer after T004.
Serial owner of fast-lane test selection output. Must complete before modes.ts consumes the new impact signal.
Serial modes.ts join. Must not run in parallel with T008, T010, T015, or T020 if any are ready.
[P] marker is unsafe unless T007 is complete and no other modes.ts task is ready. Prefer making T008 serial or placing it behind a modes.ts join.
Read-only measurement task. Can run only after T008 and after quality:fast is available; no write conflict.
Serial PR-mode join after T007 and T008.
Serial runner change after PR mode ordering is stable.
[P] marker is unsafe as written because types.ts is a shared surface with T003, T016, and T019. Keep as serial type join or merge type additions into one types.ts task.
Serial after RiskLevel type and T002. Isolated from impact-report and modes, but it produces inputs consumed by T014.
Serial after T013 and T006. Conflicts with T006 on impact-report.ts if scheduled too early.
Serial modes.ts join after risk output exists.
[P] marker is unsafe as written because types.ts is shared with T003, T012, and T019. Prefer a single type-surface join before runner/reporter work.
Serial runner change after T016 and T011. Conflicts with T011 if batched early.
Isolated reporter change after runner report fields exist.
[P] marker is unsafe as written because types.ts is shared with T003, T012, and T016. Prefer serial type-surface consolidation.
Serial modes.ts coverage-mode join after T019 and T010.
Serial runner coverage behavior after T020 and T017.
Single shared routing test file. Must be serial create-then-append tasks or split into isolated test files per concern.
Read-only final validation. Must run after all implementation and test tasks.
Artifact-only documentation update after validation results exist. Must not touch source or tests.

parallel_batches.notes: Both tasks are read-only and can run together. Join before any implementation task consumes pattern findings.
T003 is a single types.ts edit. T005 cannot run in parallel with T004 because it depends on T004; current batch wording should be corrected.
T012 touches shared types.ts and T013 depends on T012, so Phase 5 has no safe parallel pair as written.
T016 touches shared types.ts and T017 depends on T016, so Phase 6 has no safe parallel pair as written.
T019 touches shared types.ts and T020 depends on T019, so Phase 7 has no safe parallel pair as written.
T012, T016, and T019 conflict on types.ts; only T006 is isolated. Use serial type join before parallel downstream work.

parallel_batches.batch: Potential corrected batch after T004 and T006
parallel_batches.notes: Both tasks modify modes.ts. Add a serial modes.ts join or combine lane-definition edits.

conflicts.resolution: Recommend one serial type-surface join task that adds QualityGateMode fast, RiskLevel, report fields, and coverageMode, followed by dependent implementation tasks.
Keep modes.ts edits serial or split only if each task edits a disjoint exported definition and a join task owns final mode ordering.
Keep runner.ts edits serial because lane filtering, report fields, and coverage flags share execution flow.
Keep T014 after T006; both modify report semantics.

Serial append chain is safe. If parallelism is desired, split by file: fast-mode-routing.test.ts, fast-lane-selection.test.ts, risk-escalation.test.ts, output-semantics.test.ts, coverage-mode.test.ts.
Single owner. Treat package scripts as forbidden to every other task.

forbidden_scope_defaults: package.json unless the task write_scope is exactly package.json
scripts/quality-gate/types.ts unless explicitly owned by the task
scripts/quality-gate/modes.ts unless explicitly owned by the task
scripts/quality-gate/runner.ts unless explicitly owned by the task
scripts/quality-gate/reporter.ts unless explicitly owned by the task
scripts/pr/impact-report.ts unless explicitly owned by the task
scripts/pr/change-policy.ts unless explicitly owned by the task
scripts/quality-gate/__tests__/routing.test.ts unless explicitly owned by the task

verification_adjustments.issue: runner.test.ts may not compile until modes.ts understands fast mode if tests import exhaustive QualityGateMode assumptions.
verification_adjustments.adjustment: Use a typecheck or defer runner.test.ts until T004; otherwise make T003 part of the serial type/mode join.

verification_adjustments.issue: bun run quality:fast --dry-run is non-runnable before T005 creates the script and before QualityGateMode accepts fast.
verification_adjustments.adjustment: Keep as post-T005 verification only; do not use this command in earlier tasks.

verification_adjustments.issue: time bun run quality:fast --dry-run is not portable in the declared PowerShell environment.
verification_adjustments.adjustment: Use PowerShell Measure-Command or a Bun/Node timing wrapper, for example Measure-Command { bun run quality:fast --dry-run }.

verification_adjustments.issue: bun run quality:fast --dry-run checks command execution but may not assert reporter banner content unless output is inspected or tested.
verification_adjustments.adjustment: Add/require a reporter or routing test assertion for DEVELOPMENT SIGNAL, NOT PR READY, PR READINESS, and ESCALATED CHECKS.

verification_adjustments.issue: coverage.test.ts alone may not prove runner passes --changed based on lane coverageMode.
verification_adjustments.adjustment: Run or add focused runner coverage-mode tests, e.g. bun test scripts/quality-gate/runner.test.ts scripts/quality-gate/coverage.test.ts.

verification_adjustments.issue: tasks reference scripts/quality-gate/__tests__/routing.test.ts, but existing tests in this area are currently colocated as scripts/quality-gate/*.test.ts.
verification_adjustments.adjustment: Either create the __tests__ directory intentionally and use that path consistently, or follow existing colocated test pattern with scripts/quality-gate/routing.test.ts.

verification_adjustments.issue: bun test scripts/quality-gate/__tests__/routing.test.ts does not produce an 80% coverage report by itself.
verification_adjustments.adjustment: If 80% coverage is a hard acceptance criterion, add bun run check:coverage or a coverage-specific command that emits the coverage report.

verification_adjustments.issue: Final validation omits bun run verify even though acceptance requires verify completes for low-risk changes.
verification_adjustments.adjustment: Add bun run verify or explicitly document why non-live PR gate cannot be run.

task_adjustments: Remove [P] from T008, T012, T016, and T019 unless task generation introduces disjoint files or a serial type/modes join that makes them isolated in their ready batch.
Correct Batch 2 wording: T005 cannot run in parallel with T004 after T004 completes.
Replace Phase 5/6/7 parallel claims with serial joins or a single combined types.ts task followed by separate implementation tasks.
Add explicit serial join tasks for types.ts, modes.ts, runner.ts, package.json script registration, and routing test file ownership.
Consider splitting routing tests into multiple files if task parallelism is more important than a single consolidated test file.
Add package.json to forbidden paths for all tasks except T005.
Add task-generation and task-packet artifact paths to forbidden scopes for all implementation packets.
validation_evidence: package.json currently defines quality:gate, quality:pr, quality:baseline, quality:release, and quality:smoke; it does not yet define quality:fast.
scripts/quality-gate/modes.ts currently filters lanes by requiredForModes and has shared mode/lane composition in one file.
scripts/quality-gate/runner.ts currently owns filterLanesForOptions, impactRequiredCheck skipping, report assembly, and coverage summary parsing.
scripts/pr/impact-report.ts accepts --files as a comma-separated CLI argument and emits Required local checks, Test coverage signals, and Risk notes sections.
scripts/pr/change-policy.ts currently keeps areasForPath internal, so T006 may need an export or alternate public helper before impact-report can use it directly.
Existing quality-gate tests are colocated under scripts/quality-gate/*.test.ts; no scripts/quality-gate/__tests__/routing.test.ts exists in the inspected tree.
concerns: T006 acceptance says use areasForPath() from change-policy.ts, but areasForPath is not exported in the inspected file; the task must either export it with tests or use evaluateChangePolicy() output.
The current task graph overuses [P] on shared type and mode surfaces; downstream executors could race or duplicate edits if they trust the markers literally.
The plan estimates 8-12 affected files, but tasks centralize many changes in a few shared files, making serial coordination more important than broad parallelism.
recovery_hints: Regenerate the task package with a serial shared-surface foundation: types.ts first, modes.ts second, runner.ts third, then isolated reporter/impact/change-policy/test lanes.
If preserving parallelism, split tests by concern and avoid multiple workers owning the same test file.
Treat every [P] marker as provisional until the ready batch is listed with non-overlapping write_scope values.
